mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return (p > 0.05) over a 10-year backtest, or decoupling of RSAFS and HOUST growth yields identical market outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the industrial production index signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns during post-recession recovery phases, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue fundamentals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of high-inflation months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth is driven by volatile energy components.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates a tightening labor market meeting restrictive monetary policy, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when both series trend upward, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in retail sales growth below 0.2% signals weakening consumer spending momentum, which immediately translates to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of low-growth retail sales months, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.
